description: Swagger Petstore sample API for managing pets, stores, and users.

endpoints.description: Updates an existing pet by Id. Requires a full Pet object in the request body. Returns the updated Pet object. Edge cases include invalid or missing fields, nonexistent pets, or validation errors.

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with id, name, photoUrls, category, tags, and status.

endpoints.description: Creates a new pet in the store. Requires a Pet object in the request body. Edge cases include missing required fields or invalid structure.

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with name and photoUrls required.

endpoints.description: Returns pets filtered by status. Multiple comma-separated statuses allowed.
Returns pets filtered by tags. Query accepts comma-separated list.
Returns a single pet. Supports authentication via api_key or OAuth2.

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of the pet to return.

endpoints.summary: Update a pet with form data.
endpoints.description: Updates specific fields of a pet using form data via query parameters.
Deletes a pet by ID. Optional api_key header may be used.
Uploads an image file for a pet. Accepts binary data in request body.
Returns inventory counts by status.
Creates an order for a pet.

request_body.schema_summary: Order object containing id, petId, quantity, shipDate, status, complete.

endpoints.description: Returns order if ID <= 5 or > 10. Other values return errors.
Deletes order with ID < 1000.
Creates a user. Requires a full User object.

request_body.schema_summary: User object with id, username, firstName, lastName, email, password, phone, userStatus.

endpoints.description: Creates users from an input array.
Logs user in. Returns a session token string.
Logs out current user.
Returns user details by username.
Updates a user by username.

request_body.schema_summary: User object with fields to update.

endpoints.description: Deletes a user by username.

description: Sample Petstore API for managing pets, store orders, inventory, and users.

endpoints.description: Update an existing pet by Id. The full Pet object must be provided and will replace the existing record. If the pet ID does not exist, the behavior may depend on implementation (often treated as not found) (inferred).

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with id, name, category, photoUrls, tags, and status.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the updated Pet object.
200.schema_summary: Pet object representing the updated resource.

400.description: Invalid ID supplied or request body failed basic validation (inferred).

example.detail: Pet id must be a positive integer.

404.description: Pet not found for the provided ID.

endpoints.summary: Add a new pet to the store.
endpoints.description: Create a new pet in the store. The Pet object should include at least name and photoUrls; id may be supplied by client or generated by the server (inferred).

request_body.schema_summary: Pet object with required fields name and photoUrls, plus optional id, category, tags, and status.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the created Pet object, potentially with server-assigned id.
200.schema_summary: Pet object representing the newly created resource.

example.detail: Field 'photoUrls' must contain at least one URL.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of pets filtered by their status. Multiple status values can be provided as a comma-separated string, e.g. 'available,pending'. If omitted, defaults to 'available'.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status values that need to be considered for filter. Multiple values can be comma-separated.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns an array of Pet objects matching the supplied status filters.

example.detail: Status must be one of: available, pending, sold.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of pets filtered by one or more tags. Multiple tags can be provided with comma-separated strings, e.g. 'tag1,tag2,tag3'.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Tags to filter by. Multiple tags can be specified using comma separation.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns an array of Pet objects that have at least one of the specified tags (implementation-specific matching, inferred).

example.detail: At least one tag must be provided.

endpoints.description: Returns a single pet for the given petId. If the ID is invalid or the pet does not exist, an error is returned.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the Pet object.
200.schema_summary: Pet object corresponding to the requested id.

example.detail: petId must be a positive integer.

endpoints.summary: Update a pet in the store with form data.
endpoints.description: Updates a pet resource based on simple form-like parameters (name and/or status). Only the provided fields are updated; unspecified fields remain unchanged (inferred).

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of pet that needs to be updated.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New status of the pet (available, pending, or sold).

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the updated Pet object.

example.detail: Status must be one of: available, pending, sold.

endpoints.description: Delete a pet identified by petId. Optionally, an api_key header may be supplied for API key–based authorization (inferred).

parameters.name: api_key
parameters.description: Optional API key for additional authorization.

example.detail: petId must be a positive integer.

endpoints.description: Upload an image file associated with the specified petId. The file is sent as raw binary in the request body with content type application/octet-stream. Optional additionalMetadata can be provided as a query parameter.

parameters.name: petId
parameters.description: ID of pet to update with the uploaded image.

request_body.schema_summary: Binary content of the image file.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns an ApiResponse with upload status.
200.schema_summary: ApiResponse object containing code, type, and message.

endpoints.description: Returns a map of status codes to quantities. Keys are status values (e.g. available, pending, sold) and values are integer counts of pets in that status.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns an object mapping status strings to integer quantities.
200.schema_summary: Object whose keys are status values and values are int32 quantities.

endpoints.summary: Place an order for a pet.
endpoints.description: Place a new order in the store for a specific pet. The Order object should specify petId, quantity, and optionally status and shipDate.

request_body.schema_summary: Order object with id, petId, quantity, shipDate, status, and complete flag.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the created Order object.
200.schema_summary: Order object representing the placed order.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a purchase order by its ID. For valid responses, try integer IDs with value <= 5 or > 10. Other values will generate exceptions (demo behavior).

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: ID of order that needs to be fetched.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the Order object.
200.schema_summary: Order object corresponding to the requested orderId.

default.description: Unexpected error (including demo exceptions for disallowed IDs).

endpoints.description: Delete a purchase order by its ID. For valid responses, try integer IDs with value < 1000. Anything above 1000 or non-integers will generate API errors (demo behavior).

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: ID of the order that needs to be deleted.

example.detail: orderId must be an integer less than 1000.

endpoints.description: Create a new user. This can only be done by a logged-in user according to the description, but no explicit security scheme is attached in the spec for this endpoint (inferred).

request_body.schema_summary: User object with id, username, firstName, lastName, email, password, phone, and userStatus.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the created User object.
200.schema_summary: User object representing the created user.

endpoints.summary: Creates list of users with given input array.
endpoints.description: Create multiple users in a single request by providing an array of User objects.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns a representative User object (implementation-specific which one) (inferred).
200.schema_summary: User object, often one of the created users (inferred).

endpoints.description: Log a user into the system by providing username and password as query parameters. Returns a session token or message as a string and includes rate limit and expiry headers.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: The password for login in clear text.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns a string message or token. Response headers indicate rate limit and token expiry.
200.schema_summary: String value such as a session token or login confirmation.

endpoints.summary: Logs out current logged in user session.
endpoints.description: Log the current user out of the system. Typically invalidates the user session or token (inferred).
Retrieve a user detail based on username.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The name that needs to be fetched. Use user1 for testing.

200.description: Successful operation. Returns the User object.
200.schema_summary: User object corresponding to the requested username.

endpoints.description: Update an existing user identified by username. This can only be done by the logged-in user. The request body contains the new User representation.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Name of the user that needs to be updated.

200.schema_summary: Optional status message or empty body (inferred).

endpoints.description: Delete a user identified by username. This can only be done by the logged-in user.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The name of the user that needs to be deleted.
